2019-05-25.abstract: The divisive, charismatic Mr. Johnson, the ex-foreign secretary, is the leading candidate to succeed Mrs. May as Britain’s prime minister. The only person who can stop him may be Mr. Johnson himself.

2019-05-25.title: ‘Not Just a Maid’: The Ultra-Running Domestic Workers of Hong Kong
2019-05-25.abstract: Trail running has emerged as an unlikely equalizer for some foreign domestic workers in a city that often discriminates against them.

2019-05-25.title: In Parliament Elections, Populists Seek to Break the E.U. From Within
2019-05-25.abstract: Not long ago, nationalist movements pushed for a departure from the European Union. Now they are seeking seats in Parliament, promising an insurgency from within.

2019-05-26.title: Trump Opens Tokyo Visit With a Tweet Sure to Unnerve the Japanese
2019-05-26.abstract: President Trump played down North Korea’s recent tests of short-range ballistic missiles, undercutting declarations by both Prime Minister Shinzo Abe and the president’s own national security adviser.

2019-05-26.title: What to Watch For in the European Parliament Election Results
2019-05-26.abstract: More than 400 million Europeans are eligible to vote in an election that will measure the strength of populists — and of supporters of the European Union.

2019-05-26.title: Militants Attack Soldiers in Southern Philippines, Leaving 2 Children Dead
2019-05-26.abstract: Six militants from the insurgent group Abu Sayyaf were also killed in the gun battle on the island of Jolo.

2019-05-26.title: Peter O’Neill Resigns as Prime Minister of Papua New Guinea
2019-05-26.abstract: Mr. O’Neill’s departure after almost eight years in power comes at a sensitive moment, with China and the West fighting for influence across the region.

2019-05-26.title: Ireland Votes Overwhelmingly to Ease Divorce Restrictions
2019-05-26.abstract: With the change approved by 82 percent of voters, the country is taking another step toward liberalizing a Constitution dominated by the teachings of the Catholic Church.

2019-05-27.title: Nigel Farage’s Populist Brexit Party Wins Big in European Parliament Elections
2019-05-27.abstract: The party’s strong performance humiliated Britain’s governing Conservatives, whose leader, Prime Minister Theresa May, on Friday announced her resignation.

2019-05-27.title: Spain’s Socialists Make Gains in 3 Elections
2019-05-27.abstract: The Spanish Socialists’s strong showing strengthened the hand of Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez as he seeks to form a new government.

2019-05-27.title: 30 Dead and 200 Missing in Congo After Boat Sinks
2019-05-27.abstract: The boat sank in Lake Mai-Ndombe, hundreds of miles northeast of the capital, Kinshasa. Many of its passengers were said to be teachers.

2019-05-27.title: She Thought She’d Married a Rich Chinese Farmer. She Hadn’t.
2019-05-27.abstract: Pakistan has charged dozens of people with trafficking women to China as wives. Rabia Kanwal was one bride who says she was lied to. In China, her husband has a different story.

2019-05-27.title: Trump and Abe’s ‘Unshakable Bond’ Shows Some Cracks in Tokyo
2019-05-27.abstract: Prime Minister Shinzo Abe said he was “completely on the same page” with President Trump on North Korea and trade, but the president made it clear that being in lock step with him is a precarious position.

2019-05-28.title: Attacks by Extremists on Afghan Schools Triple, Report Says
2019-05-28.abstract: The surge, not seen since 2015, was yet another sign of the deteriorating security situation across Afghanistan.

2019-05-28.title: 18 Schoolchildren Stabbed, and Girl and Man Killed, in Attack in Japan
2019-05-28.abstract: A man carried out the attack before fatally slashing himself in the neck. NHK, Japan’s public broadcaster, said two adults were stabbed alongside the 18 girls.

2019-05-28.title: Prison Fighting Leaves at Least 55 Dead in Northern Brazil
2019-05-28.abstract: The outbreak of violence in the state of Amazonas comes as rival drug factions vie for dominance of lucrative smuggling routes.

2019-05-28.title: Japan Rolled Out the Red Carpet. Trump Veered Off Into Personal Fixations.
2019-05-28.abstract: In Japan, President Trump acted like a man who could never be fully present, defining his trip more by a focus on politics at home than diplomacy abroad, expressed daily on Twitter.

2019-05-28.title: Memories of Massacres Were Long Suppressed Here. Tourists Now Retrace the Atrocities.
2019-05-28.abstract: Jeju Island’s natural beauty has long attracted millions of South Koreans. Visitors now come to tour sites associated with a government-led postwar slaughter that was forbidden to discuss for decades.

2019-05-29.title: Denmark Rejects Final Asylum Appeal by Afghan Woman With Dementia
2019-05-29.abstract: The government says Zarmena Waziri, 72, has not provided adequate proof of her illness. She was ordered to report to a deportation center on June 4.

2019-05-29.title: Bolton Says Iran Is Likely Responsible for Oil Tanker Attacks
2019-05-29.abstract: John R. Bolton, President Trump’s national security adviser, accused Iran on Wednesday of being behind reported attacks on ships off the coast of the United Arab Emirates.

2019-05-29.title: Britain Vowed Big Changes After Grenfell Tower Burned. Why Are Thousands Stuck in Firetraps?
2019-05-29.abstract: Two years after the Grenfell inferno in London killed 72 people, hundreds of high-rise apartment buildings in England remain wrapped in flammable cladding — and safety rules are little changed.

2019-05-29.title: Nepal Says Everest Rules Might Change After Traffic Jams and Deaths
2019-05-29.abstract: Officials said that climbers might have to submit proof of mountaineering experience and a verifiable certificate of good health.

2019-05-29.title: John Bercow Vows to Stay as Speaker, Asserting Parliament’s Brexit Role
2019-05-29.abstract: The possibility that Britain’s next prime minister might lead the country out of the European Union without a withdrawal deal sets up a constitutional confrontation.

2019-05-30.title: Bus With Mexican Catholic Pilgrims Collides With Tractor-Trailer, Killing 21
2019-05-30.abstract: A bus carrying members of a Catholic archdiocese struck a tractor-trailer and burst into flames on a highway between the cities of Puebla and Orizaba.

2019-05-30.title: U.S.-Funded Afghan College Is Under Scrutiny for Missing Millions, Officials Say
2019-05-30.abstract: The American University of Afghanistan is at risk of closing after United States government investigators said they could not account for $63 million in spending.

2019-05-30.title: ‘Why Are So Many of Our Girls Dying?’ Canada Grapples With Violence Against Indigenous Women
2019-05-30.abstract: As violence against Indigenous women and girls grows, a national inquiry into the causes is drawing to a close, with a report expected on June 3.

2019-05-30.title: Modi Begins Second Term as India’s Prime Minister After Crushing Victory
2019-05-30.abstract: As the world watches to see how Mr. Modi uses his electoral landslide, a huge swearing-in ceremony, featuring about 8,000 guests, gave a first look.

2019-05-30.title: In Israel’s New Election Campaign, Right Battles Right
2019-05-30.abstract: The fight between the secular right and the religious right thwarted Benjamin Netanyahu’s attempt to form a government and has eclipsed the old left-right divide.

2019-05-31.title: Seeing China Through Art, Not Politics
2019-05-31.abstract: For those of us hungering for deeper insight into the the country, Sydney’s White Rabbit gallery provides a dizzying and confronting lesson in understanding.

2019-05-31.title: North Korea Executed and Purged Top Nuclear Negotiators, South Korean Report Says
2019-05-31.abstract: The report comes amid speculation that the North’s leader is changing tack after his summit meeting with President Trump failed to lift punishing sanctions.

2019-05-31.title: New Documents Show Power Games Behind China’s Tiananmen Crackdown
2019-05-31.abstract: A book released in Hong Kong reveals how after the 1989 massacre, China’s top officials united to denounce an ousted leader and embrace a darkly wary worldview.

2019-05-31.title: Pope Francis, in Romania, Warns of Populism’s Dangers
2019-05-31.abstract: In a three-day trip to the former communist country, the pope will seek to heal an ancient schism between the Catholic and Orthodox churches.

2019-05-31.title: Dutch Man, Captive for 7 Years, Dies as Philippines Troops Try to Free Him
2019-05-31.abstract: The hostage, Ewold Horn, had been taken hostage by Abu Sayyaf militants in 2012 while he was bird-watching on a remote southern island.
